deliverables: Design narrative addressing public program, community benefit, and civic character (2–4 pages)
Preliminary floor plan sketches (1:200 scale minimum)
Site placement / orientation / public access concept
3D conceptual massing model
Material and finish direction board (durability, maintainability, and public character emphasis)
Space arrangement diagram showing all programmed public and administrative spaces
Accessibility compliance concept (full accessibility from arrival to all public areas)
Sustainability concept (passive design strategies; solar systems required per contracting entity)
scope_phases.milestone_trigger: Phase 1 complete when contracting entity's authorized representative provides written approval of conceptual direction (may require technical committee approval).
scope_phases.revision_assumptions: 2 rounds of government entity revision included. Each additional round: MXN 10,000/day. Government review cycles are often longer than private client cycles.

deliverables: Floor plans all levels (1:100 scale)
Elevations all facades (1:100)
Building sections minimum 3 (1:100)
Roof plan
Site plan with property boundaries, setbacks, public access routes, accessible parking, service entry
Interior design criteria document (public durability standards, accessibility integration, civic identity)
Accessibility compliance matrix (full NOM-001-STPS and IMSS/ISSSTE accessibility standards; all public areas)
Window and door schedule
Preliminary structural coordination sketch
Sustainability compliance strategy document (passive design measures, envelope performance; solar systems included per program requirement)
Landscape architecture — pedestrian areas, accessible routes, planting zones, hardscape (included by default for public civic projects)
Irrigation plan (included by default for public civic projects)
collaborators_required: Structural engineer (Oficio Taller coordinates; contract structure per government procurement rules)
Landscape architect (Oficio Taller coordinates; included by default)
scope_phases.milestone_trigger: Phase 2 complete when contracting entity's authorized representative provides written approval (may require technical and budget committee approval).
scope_phases.revision_assumptions: 2 rounds of government entity revision included. Each additional round: MXN 10,000/day.

deliverables: Structural engineering drawings integrated with architectural set
Electrical single-line diagram and panel schedules (including public safety lighting, emergency systems)
Lighting layout plan (interior and exterior public area lighting; energy efficiency compliance)
Plumbing and hydraulic layout (including public restrooms at code-required counts and accessibility standards)
HVAC system design (public occupancy ventilation standards; Cabo San Lucas climate)
Landscape engineering coordination (grading, drainage, irrigation system design)
Solar systems design (in scope — required by contracting entity per program)
Foundation plan and details
Sustainability coordination: confirm all passive design measures implemented in engineering systems
scope_phases.revision_assumptions: 1 round of coordination revision included. Changes driven by program additions after Phase 2 approval are billed as change orders.

deliverables: Fully coordinated construction drawing set (architectural + structural + MEP + landscape)
Detail sheets: wall sections, public restroom details, accessible route details, entrance canopy, site furniture
Finish schedule (all surfaces, all areas — public durability standards throughout)
Door and window schedule with specifications
Site furniture and signage coordination (benches, bollards, bicycle parking, wayfinding) — specification level
Technical specifications document (government procurement-compatible format)
Sustainability certification documentation package (solar systems as applicable)
Public bidding technical documents (engineering quantities and specifications formatted for licitación pública)
collaborators_required: All engineers from Phase 3 must deliver coordinated final drawings
scope_phases.revision_assumptions: 1 round of coordination revision included. Changes after executive plan approval require a change order (subject to government change order process).

deliverables: Technical bid package (executive plans + technical specifications + engineering quantities — formatted for government licitación)
Bid comparison technical review (Oficio Taller reviews technical submissions; contracting entity runs the formal procurement process)
Technical recommendation with rationale
scope_phases.duration_weeks: 6–12 (public procurement process timeline — contracting entity manages)
scope_phases.note: For public civic projects, the formal contractor bidding process (licitación pública) is governed by the contracting entity under applicable procurement law. Oficio Taller prepares and provides technical documents. The contracting entity manages the formal procurement process, evaluates bids against legal/financial criteria, and makes the contractor selection. Minimum bid count is set by the contracting entity per obra pública procurement rules.

deliverables: Building permit application package
Submission to municipal authority (Municipio de Los Cabos)
Any additional government authorizations required
Tracking and correction management until all permits issued
scope_phases.note: Government-commissioned projects: inter-agency coordination may add complexity. Public project permit process is expected to be longer than residential projects. Oficio Taller will navigate the applicable process.

payment_schedule.name: Contract Signing
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Signed contract (per government contracting process) + first payment authorized

payment_schedule.name: Concept Design Approved
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Contracting entity's written approval of Phase 1 (may require technical committee approval)

payment_schedule.name: Architectural Design Approved
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Contracting entity's written approval of Phase 2 (may require technical and budget committee approval)

payment_schedule.name: Construction Documents Delivered
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Executive plans package and public bid documents delivered to contracting entity

payment_schedule.name: Construction Start Authorized
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Building permit obtained and contracting entity authorizes construction start

payment_schedule_note: 20/15/20/25/20 split per public_civic template. Total architecture fee: $180,000 USD per seed data. Government payment terms subject to fiscal year budget availability and contracting entity's standard payment authorization process.
exclusions: Structural, electrical, plumbing, civil, and landscape engineering fees (coordinated by Oficio Taller; contract structure per government procurement rules)
Permit fees, municipal taxes, government charges, environmental impact study fees
Formal licitación pública procurement management (Oficio Taller provides technical documents; contracting entity runs the process)
Site furniture, signage, and wayfinding procurement (Oficio Taller specifies; contracting entity procures)
Topographic survey (required — contracting entity responsibility to provide)
Environmental impact study (if required — contracting entity responsibility, or add as service)
Archaeological survey (if required in project area — contracting entity responsibility)
Legal review of property title or government land use authorization
Community engagement process (if required — add as service)
LEED / sustainability certification fees and third-party submission management (Oficio Taller prepares documentation; certification submission fees are contracting entity's responsibility)
Road, highway, or large-scale civil infrastructure design (civil engineering exclusion)
Construction supervision / Phase 7 (not included unless contracting entity includes supervision in contract scope — note: government contracts frequently include a supervision requirement)

revision_assumptions.phase_1: 2 rounds of government entity revision included. Each additional round: MXN 10,000/day. Government approval cycles may be longer than private client cycles — milestone approval may require technical committee sign-off.
revision_assumptions.phase_2: 2 rounds of government entity revision included. Each additional round: MXN 10,000/day. Milestone approval may require technical and budget committee approval.
revision_assumptions.phase_3: 1 round of coordination revision included. Changes driven by program additions after Phase 2 approval are billed as change orders.
revision_assumptions.phase_4: 1 round of coordination revision included. Changes after executive plan approval require a change order (subject to government change order process).

project_type_clauses.title: Government Procurement Framework
project_type_clauses.text: This project is commissioned by a government entity and is subject to applicable federal or state public procurement regulations (Ley de Adquisiciones, Arrendamientos y Servicios del Sector Público or state equivalent). Oficio Taller will fulfill its design and technical obligations in accordance with the terms of the government contract. Requirements arising from the procurement framework that expand the scope of design services beyond those described in this document will be addressed as change orders.

project_type_clauses.title: Public Contractor Bidding Process
project_type_clauses.text: The contractor bidding process for this project is a formal licitación pública managed by the contracting entity under applicable procurement law. Oficio Taller prepares the technical documents (plans, specifications, engineering quantities) that form the technical section of the bid package. Oficio Taller provides a technical recommendation on contractor submissions. The contracting entity has sole authority to select the contractor. Oficio Taller is not responsible for contractor selection outcomes or for contractor performance.

project_type_clauses.title: Government Approval Cycles
project_type_clauses.text: This project requires approval from the contracting entity's authorized representatives, which may include technical committees, budget committees, and elected governing bodies. Approval timelines between phases are outside Oficio Taller's control. Project timeline milestones are set relative to approval events, not calendar dates. Delays caused by government approval processes do not constitute breaches of Oficio Taller's obligations under this contract.

project_type_clauses.title: Construction Supervision
project_type_clauses.text: Construction supervision (Phase 7) is an optional service for this project. Government contracts frequently include a supervision scope. If supervision is included, Oficio Taller will produce documentation in the format required by the contracting entity, including the bitácora de obra. If supervision is not included in the contract scope, the contracting entity is responsible for construction oversight and compliance verification.

project_type_clauses.title: Electronic Signature
project_type_clauses.text: This document is valid when signed electronically by the client and an authorized Oficio Taller representative. Electronic signatures carry the same legal weight as physical signatures under Mexican federal law (NOM-151-SCFI).
